metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC5 that verifies the ADR file exists and contains the required experiment figures (e.g., `grep -qF 'recall@20 = 1.0' docs/adr/0037-cross-ticket-overlap.md && grep -qF '0.69 ms' docs/adr/0037-cross-ticket-overlap.md`).
findings.finding: AC5 (ADR deliverable) has no concrete proving command. The acceptance criterion says 'The ADR docs/adr/0037-cross-ticket-overlap.md is written, citing the concrete experiment figures' but no pytest test, grep command, or file-existence check is named — leaving 'done' unverifiable by CI or a deterministic command.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC5: 'The ADR docs/adr/0037-cross-ticket-overlap.md is written (epic's ADR deliverable), citing the concrete experiment figures...'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC5: 'Proven by: `test -f docs/adr/0037-cross-ticket-overlap.md && grep -qF "recall@20 = 1.0" docs/adr/0037-cross-ticket-overlap.md && grep -qF "0.69 ms" docs/adr/0037-cross-ticket-overlap.md && grep -qF "1.90" docs/adr/0037-cross-ticket-overlap.md`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the `LLMConfig` field for `overlap_conf_threshold` (e.g. `overlap_confidence_threshold`), provide its default value and valid range, and cite the story that defines it — or add it directly to this story's scope if it is not defined elsewhere.
findings.finding: `overlap_conf_threshold` is referenced in the aggregation rule as the confidence gate (`≥overlap_conf_threshold`) but its `LLMConfig` field name, default value, and valid range are never defined in this plan — the plan only says 'all tunables live on `LLMConfig` per the other stories', without naming which story or which field.
findings.location: What section — aggregation rule: `both orderings agree + cited artifact + ≥overlap_conf_threshold`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define precisely what 'both orderings agree' means — e.g., 'both calls return the same `relation` value AND both cite at least one common artifact AND both confidence scores meet the threshold' — and specify what to emit when orderings disagree (e.g. drop the candidate silently).
findings.finding: The 'both orderings agree' aggregation protocol is structurally incomplete: the plan never specifies what 'agree' means when S6 returns successfully for both orderings (A→B and B→A) but with *different* `relation` values (e.g. A→B = `duplicates`, B→A = `supersedes`). This non-error disagreement case is distinct from the `test_runtime_error` fallback and has no specified outcome.
findings.location: What section — 'both orderings agree + cited artifact + ≥overlap_conf_threshold'; Testing section — `test_runtime_error`
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'Agreement requires: (a) same `relation` value in both orderings, (b) at least one overlapping `shared_artifact` string, and (c) both confidence scores ≥ `overlap_conf_threshold`. A candidate where the two orderings produce different `relation` values is dropped (not an error).'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve where `overlap_conf_threshold` lives: name the prior story that adds it to `LLMConfig`, or add it explicitly to this story's Scope (alongside `overlap_enabled` on `VerifyConfig`), with a rationale for why it belongs on `LLMConfig` rather than `VerifyConfig`.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `overlap_conf_threshold` lives on `LLMConfig` ('all tunables live on `LLMConfig` per the other stories'), but `LLMConfig` in the codebase has no such field. No named prior story is specified as the dependency that adds it, leaving this a dangling capability assertion that S7 depends on but cannot verify.
findings.location: What section: 'Whether a candidate is overlapping enough to appear is decided ENTIRELY by S6's aggregation rule (both orderings agree + cited artifact + ≥overlap_conf_threshold)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify that the `relation` field in S6's output schema is constrained to the 3-value enum (`duplicates|supersedes|depends_on`), OR define a mapping/filter rule in S7 for translating/dropping candidates whose relation falls outside that set before populating `link_command`.
findings.finding: The `relation` enum in the S6 output shape (`"duplicates"|"supersedes"|"depends_on"`) is a strict subset of the live `rebar link` relation vocabulary (`blocks | depends_on | relates_to | duplicates | supersedes | discovered_from`), but no prompt constraint, schema enum, or filtering rule is specified to enforce that S6 only emits values from this 3-value set — leaving the `link_command` field potentially malformed.
findings.location: What section — S6 surfaced-finding shape: `{counterpart_id: str, relation: "duplicates"|"supersedes"|"depends_on", ...}`
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the What section: 'S6's output schema constrains `relation` to `{"duplicates", "supersedes", "depends_on"}`; S7 must assert this at the S6 boundary and drop (not error) any candidate whose `relation` falls outside the set before building `link_command`.' Cross-reference the S6 story's output schema.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State explicitly which component enforces the ≤3 cap on overlap results (e.g., 'S7 slices the S6-passing set to the top 3 by confidence before attaching to overlap[]') so the executor has a single unambiguous decision point.
findings.finding: The plan states S7 'does no thresholding of its own' and that S6 decides which candidates are 'overlapping enough', yet also says S7 'attach ≤3 results to overlap[]'. It is unclear whether the ≤3 cap is enforced by S5 (retrieve returns top-3), S6 (judge returns at most 3 passing items), or S7 (slice after S6). An executing agent must decide this without guidance.
findings.location: What section: 'S6's aggregation rule (both orderings agree + cited artifact + ≥overlap_conf_threshold); S7 does no thresholding of its own' and 'attach ≤3 results to overlap[]'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence: 'S7 takes the S6-passing set sorted descending by confidence and keeps at most the top 3, then attaches them to overlap[]. S5 and S6 are not responsible for this cap.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a concrete wall-clock timeout (e.g. `overlap_timeout_s` defaulting to 5 s) for the combined S1+S5+S6 chain and state the overall latency budget for review_plan with overlap enabled.
findings.finding: The synchronous S1+S5+S6 chain executes inside the user-facing `_run_plan_review` call with no stated latency budget or timeout value/mechanism — 'timeout' is listed as a handled exception case but no actual timeout is configured, so slow LLM responses in S6 can block the CLI/MCP response indefinitely.
findings.location: What section — 'synchronously extract … run retrieve (S5), run the pairwise judge (S6)'; Gate/skip section: 'exception, timeout … → caught'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `overlap_timeout_s: float = 5.0` to LLMConfig; wrap the S1→S5→S6 call sequence in `asyncio.wait_for` or a `threading.Timer`-based deadline and catch `TimeoutError` in the same handler that catches other runtime failures.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the maximum number of S6 LLM calls per review (e.g. cap S5 candidates passed to S6 at a small fixed number, or note the expected call count given recall@20 and S6's aggregation rule) so the latency budget is explicit and implementers know whether to impose a per-candidate timeout.
findings.finding: The plan specifies S6 (pairwise judge) as a synchronous call on the `_run_plan_review` hot path but does not state how many LLM round-trips S6 may make (both orderings of each candidate pair = up to 2N calls for N candidates from S5). If S5 recall@20 returns many candidates and S6's aggregation rule requires both orderings, a high-traffic install could see multiple sequential LLM calls added to every review_plan invocation with no stated timeout budget or concurrency bound — the graceful-skip covers crashes but not latency spikes.
findings.location: What > 'run the pairwise judge (S6)' / 'Runtime failure in S1/S5/S6 (exception, timeout, empty candidates) → caught, empty overlap[], logged, never blocks'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State an explicit cap on S5 candidate count fed to S6 (e.g. top-K ≤ 10) and add a per-review-call cost estimate for S6 judge invocations to the plan.
findings.finding: The S6 pairwise judge runs 'both orderings' per candidate from S5's result set; with recall@20 the corpus can return up to 20 candidates, yielding up to 40 synchronous LLM calls per review invocation. No bound on S5 candidate count is stated, and no per-call cost estimate for S6 is provided — this is an O(candidates) LLM-call pattern with unbounded per-review economics.
findings.location: What section — 'run retrieve (S5), run the pairwise judge (S6)' + 'both orderings agree'; AC: test_surfaced
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `overlap_candidate_limit` (e.g. default 5) on LLMConfig that caps S5 results before S6, and document the resulting worst-case LLM call count (2 × limit) and estimated per-review cost in the ADR.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that `overlap[]` is injected into the verdict dict immediately before (or after) the final `findings.validate_structured` return call in `_run_plan_review`, noting that either placement is valid because `plan_review_verdict.schema.json` declares `additionalProperties: true` — so no re-validation step is needed.
findings.finding: The plan says overlap[] is injected 'AFTER sidecar.emit() and the attestation signing' but does not specify its placement relative to the final `findings.validate_structured(verdict, 'plan_review_verdict')` call, which is currently the last statement in `_run_plan_review` and returns the verdict dict. An implementer following the plan literally may inject overlap before `validate_structured` (correct — schema accepts it) or attempt to append to the returned value after the call (also works since `validate_structured` returns the same dict object), but the ambiguity could cause confusion or a double-validation if the implementer re-validates after injection.
findings.location: What > 'injection happens AFTER sidecar.emit() and the attestation signing' / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:496-501
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'Concretely, the overlap injection is a single `verdict["overlap"] = _run_overlap(...)` line inserted between the attestation signing block and the existing `return findings.validate_structured(verdict, "plan_review_verdict")` call — no second validate_structured call needed, since the schema is additionalProperties: true.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the skip/no-op path always sets `overlap: []` (empty list, key present) in the returned verdict dict, and add a `test_render` sub-case asserting the CLI produces no overlap section (or an empty one) without error when `overlap` is `[]`.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify whether `_render_plan_review_text` receives `overlap: []` (empty list) or an absent `overlap` key when the step is skipped/flag-off, nor how the renderer handles those two cases — leaving a silent KeyError or missing-section rendering gap unaddressed.
findings.suggested_fix: Define the contract: on any skip/no-op/error path, inject `result['overlap'] = []` before returning from `_run_plan_review`, so `_render_plan_review_text` can unconditionally iterate `verdict.get('overlap', [])` without KeyError. Add this invariant to the Gate/skip section and a corresponding test assertion.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC1 (and test_surfaced) to assert the complete overlap item shape — counterpart_id, relation (enum), shared_artifact, confidence (numeric), and link_command — not just link_command presence.
findings.finding: The full overlap item shape — {counterpart_id, relation, shared_artifact, confidence, link_command} — is specified in the What section, but AC1 and test_surfaced only verify the presence of a 'rebar link command' (link_command field). The other four fields are described deliverables with no acceptance criterion or test coverage.
findings.location: What section (item shape definition) vs. AC1 ('each with a rebar link command') and Testing section ('test_surfaced (≤3 in overlap[] with link commands)')
findings.suggested_fix: In AC1, replace 'each with a rebar link command' with 'each a dict with keys counterpart_id (str), relation ("duplicates"|"supersedes"|"depends_on"), shared_artifact (str), confidence (float), link_command (str)'; mirror this in the test_surfaced fixture assertion.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `src/rebar/_commands/show_config.py` (lines 75-76) as an external consumer of `_SECTIONS["verify"]` that will automatically surface `overlap_enabled` in `rebar show-config` output — confirm this is acceptable and no additional doc/help text is needed alongside the key.
findings.finding: The plan names `src/rebar/schemas/__init__.py:110` as a reference point for NO_SCHEMA_EXEMPT status, but the actual constant `PLAN_REVIEW_VERDICT` and the NO_SCHEMA_EXEMPT comment are at line 110-113. The plan also cites line `_mcp_llm.py:174` for the NO_SCHEMA_EXEMPT annotation — verified correct. However, the plan does NOT enumerate `_commands/show_config.py` as a consumer of `_SECTIONS` that will automatically surface `overlap_enabled` in `rebar show-config` output after the `_SECTIONS["verify"]` edit.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note in the Scope section: '`show_config.py` automatically picks up the new `_SECTIONS["verify"]` entry — no edit required, but the key will appear in `rebar show-config` output immediately.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm ADR number 0037 is unoccupied (the `docs/adr/` listing shows 0036→0038 with no 0037); if another ADR is in-flight for that slot, renumber this one before writing.
findings.finding: The ADR target file `docs/adr/0037-cross-ticket-overlap.md` does not exist in the repository, and the existing ADR sequence in `docs/adr/` skips from `0036-*` directly to `0038-governance-artifacts.md`, leaving a gap at 0037. The plan mandates writing this file, but its number is already claimed by the gap — an implementer must verify 0037 is truly unoccupied (not just hidden from the directory listing).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explain why the existing `scan_epics_for_spec` / `spec-alignment` reviewer (already detecting scope overlaps) is insufficient for per-ticket overlap at review time, and justify building a separate digest-BM25F pipeline rather than extending the existing mechanism.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel digest-BM25F retrieval pipeline for cross-ticket overlap detection, but never discusses the existing `scan_epics_for_spec` / `spec-alignment` reviewer (in `src/rebar/llm/spec_scan.py`) which already does batch cross-epic overlap detection using an LLM reviewer. The plan does not explain why this approach is insufficient for per-ticket-at-review-time overlap, nor whether S7 could reuse or extend it rather than building a parallel pipeline.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a paragraph to the Why section comparing S7's approach to `spec_scan`'s batch overlap detection: e.g., 'Unlike `scan_epics_for_spec` which requires an explicit spec text and runs batch-wide, S7 operates per-ticket without a spec, using digest-based retrieval to surface the closest counterparts. The batch reviewer cannot be repurposed here because [reason].' If this comparison reveals the approaches are compatible, consider whether S6's judge prompt can reuse the `spec-alignment` reviewer.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Ensure the experiment data backing the no-embeddings choice (recall@20=1.0, 0.69 ms latency, $1.90 enrichment cost) is committed to the repo (e.g., `docs/experiments/overlap-recall-experiment.md`) before or alongside the ADR, so ADR 0037's citations are repo-resident and independently verifiable.
findings.finding: The plan's core novelty justification — recall@20 = 1.0, single-query 0.69 ms @ 800 digests, ~$1.90 one-time enrichment for 793 tickets — is cited as coming from 'the epic's experiment comment', but these figures appear nowhere verifiable in the repository (not in `docs/experiments/`, not in source, not in any ADR or ticket file reachable from the repo root). The ADR (AC5) is supposed to cite these concrete figures, but the evidence backing them is not repo-resident.
findings.location: AC5 (ADR deliverable) and What section (recall experiment justification)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `docs/experiments/overlap-digest-recall.md` (or similar) containing the raw experiment results and methodology, then update AC5 to reference that file rather than 'the epic's experiment comment'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC or test assertion that the advisory findings list length (and advisory cap) is unchanged when overlap[] is populated — e.g., extend test_verdict_unchanged or add test_advisory_cap_unaffected to verify len(verdict['findings']) is identical with overlap on vs off.
findings.finding: The 'advisory cap exclusion' behavioral constraint — '≤3 overlap items never displace real plan-review advisories' (What section) — is described but has no corresponding AC or test that explicitly verifies it. If the injection accidentally touches the advisory count, no test would catch the regression.
findings.location: What section ('EXCLUDED from the advisory cap') vs. AC1 / Testing section
findings.suggested_fix: In the Testing section, extend test_verdict_unchanged to assert that verdict['findings'] (the advisory list) has the same length and contents regardless of whether overlap is populated, in addition to the byte-identity check on signed coverage/attestation.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
